subject: Mixed
topic: Classroom document templates and planter volume task

teacher_overview.title: Teacher Overview - Engine Document Samples
teacher_overview.subtitle: What this repo-generated sample pack is meant to prove
teacher_overview.directions: Use this sample pack to inspect whether the engine can produce classroom-ready student documents from structured package JSON.
teacher_overview.prompt: Check visual hierarchy, writing space, artifact-role separation, and print usability before moving into assessment implementation.
success_criteria: Student-facing documents are readable and not overcrowded.
Teacher-facing notes do not leak into student artifacts.
Graphic organizers have visible structure and enough writing space.
The math task feels like a real classroom decision, not a generic worksheet.

reading_response_template.title: Reading Response Template
reading_response_template.subtitle: Read, notice, explain, connect
reading_response_template.learning_target: I can read closely and explain my thinking with evidence.
reading_response_template.directions: Read the text or prompt. Write short, clear answers that use details from the text.
reading_response_template.prompt: Text or image prompt goes here. Students should have enough information to notice details and explain their thinking.

vocabulary.definition: a detail that supports your answer
how your evidence connects to your claim

success_criteria: I answered in complete sentences.
I used a detail from the text.
I explained why the detail matters.
I checked that my answer makes sense.

cer_organizer_template.title: CER Organizer
cer_organizer_template.subtitle: Claim, evidence, reasoning
cer_organizer_template.question: What answer can you support?
cer_organizer_template.learning_target: I can make a clear claim and support it with evidence and reasoning.
cer_organizer_template.directions: Start with a claim. Add evidence. Explain how the evidence proves or supports the claim.
success_criteria: My claim answers the question.
My evidence is specific.
My reasoning explains the connection.
I wrote clearly enough for someone else to follow.

graphic_organizer_template.title: Graphic Organizer Pack
graphic_organizer_template.subtitle: Pick the thinking layout that matches the task
graphic_organizer_template.prompt: Use one organizer to collect ideas before writing, discussing, or solving.
success_criteria: I chose a layout that matched the task.
I organized my ideas before writing.
I left enough detail to explain my thinking.

planter_volume_template.title: Planter Box Volume Decision Sheet
planter_volume_template.subtitle: Graphic Organizer - soil, water, and materials estimate
planter_volume_template.prompt: Our class is filling planter boxes. Use volume to estimate how much soil and water we need, then make a practical buying decision.
success_criteria: I used length × width × height to estimate planter volume.
I included units in my calculations.
I explained what we should actually buy or prepare.
I checked whether my answer is reasonable.

exit_reflection_template.title: Exit Ticket + Reflection
exit_reflection_template.subtitle: What did you learn, and what is your next step?
exit_reflection_template.learning_target: I can explain one thing I learned and one thing I still need to work on.
exit_reflection_template.prompt: Show one thing you learned today.
success_criteria: I named one thing I learned.
I asked one useful question or named a next step.
I was honest about my level of understanding.
